beats.activity: Wake up, shower, espresso at home
beats.reasoning: Yesterday's moderate energy (55/100) and good mood from garden time means Ellie starts the day refreshed but not rushed. Quick 30-min morning routine before heading to work.

beats.activity: Light Rail commute to Pike Place Market
beats.reasoning: Reliable 40-min light rail ride from Mount Baker to downtown; Ellie prefers this over driving on hot days to avoid stress.

beats.activity: Work: florist stall setup, arrangement prep, customer sales
beats.reasoning: Morning shift at the stall. Hot weather expected means Ellie focuses on keeping flowers hydrated and fresh; lighter foot traffic early but building toward lunch.

beats.activity: Lunch at Pike Place counter, hydration break
beats.reasoning: Heat and smoke mean Ellie stays put at market for a cool drink and light meal rather than venturing out; conserves energy on a hot day.

beats.activity: Work: afternoon rush, arrangements, customer service
beats.reasoning: Peak afternoon heat (91F by mid-afternoon) and smoke from BC wildfires—stall stays busy with Wednesday shoppers; Ellie manages heat by staying hydrated and working steadily through the rush.
40-min light rail ride back to Mount Baker; Ellie is tired from heat but energized by the prospect of evening plans.

beats.activity: Quick dinner prep and eat at home
beats.reasoning: Light, cool dinner (salad or pasta) to refuel before heading out; Ellie fuels up before the outdoor concert rather than eating at the park.

beats.activity: Short bus ride to Gas Works Park for outdoor concert
beats.reasoning: 30-min transit from Mount Baker to Wallingford; Ellie heads to the free outdoor concert at 6pm but avoids arriving too early to miss the heat of the day.

beats.activity: Outdoor concert at Gas Works Park, people-watching, music
beats.reasoning: Clear evening, cooling down after the hot day; Ellie attends the 6pm concert but keeps it to 2 hours—she values close friends over big crowds, so she soaks in the vibe without staying late into the crowded evening.

beats.activity: Bus ride home from Gas Works
beats.reasoning: 35-min transit back to Mount Baker; Ellie leaves the park before the late-night crowd surge, staying true to her preference for smaller social gatherings.

diary: Started the day early with my usual routine—espresso hit different this morning, maybe because I knew it'd be a scorcher. The Light Rail was packed, but I had my head in the game by the time I hit the stall. Wednesdays are solid at Pike Place; tourists and regulars both showed up. I did a bunch of peonies and ranunculus arrangements before lunch. The haze from those BC fires was noticeable even in the market—kind of muted the light coming through the skylights. Afternoon got busy; my hands were green by 5 PM. Grabbed dinner quick at home, then caught the bus to Gas Works for the concert. The park was absolutely rammed, but worth it. There's something about live music outdoors on a warm night that just settles you, you know? Watched the sunset paint the sky while some indie band played. Felt good to be around people without selling them anything.
thought_process: The heat kept me motivated—hot days mean people want flowers, want color, want something alive in their spaces. I skipped my usual leisurely lunch to power through afternoon arrangements because I could feel the rush building. The wildfire smoke bothered me more than I expected; it made me think about how fragile things are, which probably why I ended up going to the concert. I needed that tonight. After a long day on my feet arranging stems and dealing with customer indecision, I wanted to just exist in a crowd without performing. The concert felt like permission to be still.
world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
